heading: Powers of district court over prize property notwithstanding appeal
label: 8879 Powers of district court over prize property notwithstanding appeal
text: Notwithstanding an appeal, the district court may make and execute all necessary orders for the custody and disposal of prize property.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7679.34 U.S.C. 1147R.S. 565; R.S. 4637.and this section reflect—34 U.S.C. 1147(1) , , which repealed all laws authorizing distribution of prize proceeds to captors;Act of March 3, 1899, ch. 413, § 1330 Stat. 1007(2) Acts of , , and , , which defined the appellate jurisdiction of the United States circuit courts, and provided that the Supreme Court should not directly review district court decisions except in specified cases, not including prize cases; andMarch 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 12836 Stat. 1133February 13, 1925, ch. 229, § 143 Stat. 938(3) , , which repealed the Acts of , and , supra, but enacted similar provisions and changed the name of United States Circuit Courts of Appeals to United States Courts of Appeals for the several circuits.Act of June 25, 1948, ch. 64662 Stat. 869February 13, 1925March 3, 1911Reference to the court is omitted from and from the revised section in view of the 1948 Act.34 U.S.C. 1147
